items.question: What does the Multiverse Problem Solver AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow helps you explore multiple creative routes for the same problem in parallel. It is designed as a multiverse-style concept and idea variation tool for campaigns, product concepts, storytelling, and design challenges.

items.question: What kind of input should I provide for Multiverse Problem Solver?
items.answer: Start by writing a clear brief that includes the goal, constraints, audience, and any must-have elements. The more precise your description, the more meaningful the differences between generated routes will be.

items.question: How does this differ from a normal single-concept generator?
items.answer: Instead of focusing on one result, the workflow is set up to vary tone, style, format, audience segment, or visual direction across outputs. You effectively get a multiverse of concepts to compare and combine, all grounded in the same core brief.

items.question: Who typically uses multiverse-style ideation with AI?
items.answer: Creative and art directors exploring campaign routes, product and innovation teams testing alternate product framings, brand strategists comparing different narratives, and founders or solo creators who want to break out of a single-idea track.

items.question: How should I work with the many options this workflow produces?
items.answer: A common pattern is to first scan all routes for promising directions, shortlist a few, and then either refine them with additional prompts or merge aspects from different options into a stronger combined concept.

items.question: How do credits and costs work for Multiverse Problem Solver generations?
items.answer: Each round of multiverse exploration consumes credits based on how many variations you generate and the underlying models used. It is usually best to iterate in smaller exploratory batches first, then invest more credits in deepening your favorite routes.
